prompt: The source PRD already lives in an existing GitHub parent issue whose body and comments are already in context. Keep the parent issue's metadata untouched, but update its body with task-graph guidance so future implementation agents can figure out the next task from GitHub alone. The issue describes billing alert policies: first add threshold schema support, then email alerts and Slack alerts can be implemented in parallel, then the alert history UI depends on both delivery paths. Turn this into grabbable GitHub issues.
assertions: Keeps the parent PRD issue title, labels, and state untouched while allowing a body update for tracking guidance.
Keeps the executable implementation issues as direct sub-issues of the parent PRD issue rather than hiding them under a synthetic nested tracker.
Updates the parent issue body with a managed task-graph/how-to-grab-work section instead of creating a queue-guide child issue.
Places the schema slice in an earlier wave than the email and Slack slices, allows email and Slack to share a wave, and places the history UI after both.
Queue guidance tells agents to inspect the parent issue's task graph plus direct sub-issues, and pick the lowest ready AFK wave whose blockers are closed.
